subject: Probability: Definitions and Properties
question: An insurer offers a health plan to the employees of a large company. As part of this plan, the individual employees may choose exactly two of the supplementary coverages $A$, $B$, and $C$, or they may choose no supplementary coverage. The proportions of the company's employees that choose coverages $A$, $B$, and $C$ are $\frac{1}{4}$, $\frac{1}{3}$, and $\frac{5}{12}$ respectively. Determine the probability that a randomly chosen employee will choose no supplementary coverage.
